prediction.reasoning: The point estimate is set to the current price ($346.24) as a neutral prediction for a very short-term horizon (1 day), in the absence of specific directional catalysts. The confidence intervals are calculated by estimating a daily standard deviation (volatility) of approximately 1% of the stock price, which is common for many large-cap stocks. Assuming daily price changes follow a normal distribution, the intervals are derived using corresponding Z-scores for 50%, 80%, and 90% confidence levels. It is important to note that actual market movements can deviate significantly from these statistical predictions due to unforeseen news, market sentiment, or broader economic factors.
